title: Ratepayer Protection Pledge Gains Momentum in the US
description: The Ratepayer Protection Pledge, aimed at making artificial intelligence data centers pay more for electricity, has been signed by 23 governors and is being expanded, according to The Hill, USA Today, and The New York Times.

sources.title: Watch live: Trump gives remarks on expanding Ratepayer Protection Pledge
As A.I. Backlash Grows, Trump Says Firms Should Pay More for Electricity
23 governors sign Trump's cost pledge for data centers, backbone of AI

sections.heading: Ratepayer Protection Pledge Expansion
paragraphs: The Ratepayer Protection Pledge is an initiative that seeks to ensure that data centers, which are crucial for artificial intelligence operations, contribute more to the cost of electricity they consume. This pledge has gained significant support, with 23 governors signing it, as reported by USA Today.

sections.heading: Public Remarks on the Pledge
paragraphs: The expansion of the Ratepayer Protection Pledge was discussed in remarks given by Donald Trump, as covered live by The Hill. The event highlighted the growing concern over the financial impact of AI data centers on electricity costs and the need for these centers to bear a greater part of the expense.

sections.heading: Media Coverage and Public Interest
paragraphs: The New York Times also reported on the backlash against AI and the growing sentiment that firms should pay more for the electricity they use, particularly in the context of data centers. This has led to increased public interest and discussion on the topic, as evidenced by the trending search term 'ratepayer protection pledge' in the US.

faq.q: What is the Ratepayer Protection Pledge?
faq.a: The Ratepayer Protection Pledge is an initiative that aims to make AI data centers pay more for the electricity they consume, as reported by USA Today.

faq.q: How many governors have signed the pledge?
faq.a: According to USA Today, 23 governors have signed the Ratepayer Protection Pledge.

faq.q: Why is the pledge in the news?
faq.a: The pledge is in the news due to its expansion and the public remarks made by Donald Trump, as covered by The Hill, and the growing public interest in the financial implications of AI data centers, as reported by The New York Times.
